actual_reasoning: Strong fundamentals and decent coding, but architecture knowledge is exposure not ownership. Practical problem-solving is solid but not senior-level. Reading system design is encouraging but not yet demonstrated in depth.
critical_issues_to_catch: Kafka usage is exposure not ownership - 'The team decided to use Kafka before I joined'
Cloud infrastructure is exposure only - DevOps team owns it
Architecture knowledge lacks depth - cannot explain Kafka partitioning or consumer failure handling
N+1 fix was manager-initiated, not self-identified - ownership question
System design knowledge is in-progress self-study, not yet proven in work
expected_calibration: Strong Intermediate / Junior Senior - fundamentals are good but lacks the architecture ownership and depth expected at senior level
strengths_to_identify: HashMap explanation was detailed and accurate including Java 8 tree conversion
Sliding window coding solution was optimal O(n) with correct complexity analysis
